title: Neoschroetera glutinosa
intro: Neoschroetera glutinosa, also known as Strychnos, is a species of flowering plant in the family Loganiaceae, native to Madagascar. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and as a diuretic. The bioactive constituents of Neoschroetera glutinosa include alkaloids, particularly strychnine, which can be toxic in large quantities, as well as other compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infused as a tea, although its use is generally limited due to the potential toxicity of its alkaloid content.
taxonomy: Neoschroetera glutinosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It is native to tropical Africa. Neoschroetera glutinosa is a member of the tribe Hippomaneae, which is a subtribe of the Euphorbiaceae family. This plant is also known to be part of the larger Euphorbiaceae family, which includes spurge, euphorbia, and other related species. The specific classification of Neoschroetera glutinosa may be subject to further study and research for more detailed information.
